summary: The doc_build_status_check job failed because an upstream documentation build job reported a status of 'failure'. The status-check script evaluates whether the upstream job succeeded or was skipped, and since neither condition was met, it executed 'exit 1'.
root_cause: An upstream job in the 'Build documentation' workflow completed with status 'failure'. The doc_build_status_check job's inline shell script checks if the upstream job result equals 'success' or 'skipped'; since the result was 'failure', the else branch executed 'exit 1', causing this job to fail with exit code 1.

evidence.reason: Shows the upstream job result is literally 'failure', which makes the condition false and causes the script to fall through to the else branch.
The else branch of the status-check script explicitly exits with code 1 when the upstream job did not succeed or was not skipped.
Confirms the script exited with code 1, marking the job as failed.
Identifies this as the status-check job whose sole purpose is to gate on upstream doc build success.

suggested_fix: Investigate the upstream documentation build job that reported status 'failure' to find the actual build error. Fix the underlying doc build failure so that the upstream job completes with 'success', which will allow the doc_build_status_check condition to pass.
